issue_title: Unable to access S3 object with a name not conforming the regex
issue_body:
I am trying to access S3 object which address is like this
"bucket-name/data/organization_id=111111/".

In any way I tried to reach it with boto3 I got
```
Invalid bucket name "bucket-name/data/organization_id=111111/": Bucket name must match the regex "^[a-zA-Z0-9.\-_]{1,255}$"
```

The real problem lies in the fact that I do not have read rights to either "bucket-name" or "bucket-name/data". I can access only the full path.
```

botocore.exceptions.ClientError: An error occurred (AccessDenied) when calling the ListObjectsV2 operation: Access Denied
```

Is there any way to this at all with boto3?
If not at the moment, it would be an awesome thing to have.

For a project I am working on I need to access the ARN of all aws resources that support having one.
I have made an implementation in my own fork of boto3 for my own use, but was wondering if this is something that is being worked on internally?
If not is likely to be accepted as a pull request or does it change the public API too much?

Main implementation can be found here.
https://github.com/Posnet/boto3/blob/develop/boto3/resources/factory.py#L148

The general functionality is that if a resource has an '**arn**' property, it ignore it, if it has an arn but its called something like '**LoadBalancerArn**' it creates an '**arn**' alias to that property, and if it doesn't have an arn attribute, you can specify the arn via a python format string

e.g. https://github.com/Posnet/boto3/blob/develop/boto3/data/s3/2006-03-01/resources-1.json#L44

I will continue to work on my own implementation in any case, but would like to submit a PR to add support for this feature if it is likely to get accepted. (So I don't have to maintain my own fork)

The above examples all succeed under Python 2.7.

issue_body:

            'Owner': {
                'DisplayName': 'string',
                'ID': 'string'
            }

Origin of bucket data
----------------------

At the S3 console I created a bucket in four different regions and uploaded a file into each, then uses python to test a few s3 functions.  The list_objects_v2() call for one region returned an Owner object with no DisplayName field.  The list_object() and cli 'aws s3api' also return incorrect results for one bucket.  Here is code snippet with some fields removed.

[issue-py.txt](https://github.com/boto/boto3/files/1862003/issue-py.txt)
[issue-cli.txt](https://github.com/boto/boto3/files/1862002/issue-cli.txt)

TL;DR
`aws rds create-db-cluster` requires `--master-username` and `--master-user-password` when the `--replication-source-identifier` option is set. Those options are actually mutually exclusive.

I uninstalled awscli and did a forced reinstall with cache disabled, but no success
```
Successfully installed awscli-1.10.38 botocore-1.4.28 colorama-0.3.3 docutils-0.12 futures-3.0.5 jmespath-0.9.0 pyasn1-0.1.9 python-dateutil-2.5.3 rsa-3.4.2 s3transfer-0.0.1 six-1.10.0
```

# Called as specified in the aws docs.
[ec2-user@ip-OBSCURED create-rds-dr-cluster]$ aws rds create-db-cluster --region us-west-2 --db-cluster-identifier appa1p-us-east-1-rds-dr --engine aurora --replication-source-identifier arn:aws:rds:us-east-1:OBSCURED:cluster:appa1p-dbcluster-1v5y2nsaviq46
usage: aws [options] <command> <subcommand> [<subcommand> ...] [parameters]

[ec2-user@ip-OBSCURED create-rds-dr-cluster]$ aws rds create-db-cluster --region us-west-2 --db-cluster-identifier appa1p-us-east-1-rds-dr --engine aurora --replication-source-identifier arn:aws:rds:us-east-1:OBSCURED:cluster:appa1p-dbcluster-1v5y2nsaviq46 --master-username auser

```

# adding --master-user-password to complete the chain.
[ec2-user@ip-OBSCURED create-rds-dr-cluster]$ aws rds create-db-cluster --region us-west-2 --db-cluster-identifier appa1p-us-east-1-rds-dr --engine aurora --replication-source-identifier arn:aws:rds:us-east-1:OBSCURED:cluster:appa1p-dbcluster-1v5y2nsaviq46 --master-username auser --master-user-password apassword1

An error occurred (InvalidParameterCombination) when calling the CreateDBCluster operation: Cannot specify user name for read replica cluster
```

### Summary

In many clients, `NextToken` parameter is used to iterate over all resources, with say `while` loop.
However, you can't pass `NextToken` param for the first fetch, which makes codes complicated.
I believe allowing `NextToken` to be `None` will solve this.

### Example

Here's well-work example to get all cloudwatch alarms.
```python
cloudwatch = boto3.client('cloudwatch')

alarms = cloudwatch.describe_alarms() # First fetch: can't pass NextToken param
# ...
# Do something
# ...
while 'NextToken' in alarms:
    alarms = cloudwatch.describe_alarms(NextToken=alarms['NextToken'])
    # ...
    # Do same thing again
    # ...
```

If `NextToken` can be `None`, the code can be simpler:
```python
cloudwatch = boto3.client('cloudwatch')

alarms = {'NextToken': None}
while True:
    try:
        alarms = cloudwatch.describe_alarms(NextToken=alarms['NextToken'])
    except KeyError:
        break
    else:
        # ...
        # Do something
        # ...
```

Currently, above code will get following error
```
ParamValidationError: Parameter validation failed:
Invalid type for parameter NextToken, value: None, type: <type 'NoneType'>, valid types: <type 'basestring'>
```

### How to fix

For me, botocore is too complicated to make some changes, but I found following code may have something to do with the issue.

https://github.com/boto/botocore/blob/develop/botocore/client.py#L547

One idea is adding following lines:
```python
if 'NextToken' in api_params and api_params['NextToken'] is None:
    del api_params['NextToken']
```

If you tell me where to fix, I feel no reluctance in making a pull request.

### My envoironment

```python
>>> platform.system()
'Darwin'
>>> platform.mac_ver()
('10.11.3', ('', '', ''), 'x86_64')
>>> platform.python_version()
'2.7.11'
>>> botocore.__version__
'1.4.28'
>>> boto3.__version__
'1.3.1'
```

On some systems, such as EL7, dateutil 2.1 is not present, but dateutil 1.4 is present.
We have botocore running on such systems by [patching the requirement](http://pkgs.fedoraproject.org/cgit/rpms/python-botocore.git/tree/botocore-1.4.52-fix_dateutil_version.patch).
This has no side effects, as far as we saw, so I think it should be done in the upstream version as well.

reviewing the uses of `classify_field`, I discovered this: https://github.com/inspirehep/inspire-dojson/blob/a37eaa72cde753eaf828e41db96297d3fe25fe2e/inspire_dojson/hep/model.py#L51-L65

It is completely broken as:
1. `inspire_category` does not exist in the schema (`inspire_categories` is the correct form);
2. it overwrites the output on every iteration.

I don't know if it is ever run, as it requires `arxiv_eprints` to be present but `inspire_categories` not to be, which should be pretty rare, and I have never seen any migration error because of this field.

It would be good to review the whole logic around categories in dojson as it's quite intricate.

issue_title: es-visitor: make 'analyze_wildcard' parameter default to True
issue_body:
The `analyze_wildcard` flag parameter in [_generate_query_string_query](https://github.com/inspirehep/inspire-query-parser/blob/master/inspire_query_parser/visitors/elastic_search_visitor.py#L113) was introduced in case we wanted to disable wildcard analysis.

It seems that everywhere that is being used in the codebase is set to `True`, which is actually the default behaviour of ElasticSearch (see [docs](https://www.elastic.co/guide/en/elasticsearch/reference/2.4/query-dsl-query-string-query.html)).

For now it seems better to make the parameter `True` by default and if that's the case, don't generate the `"analyze_wildcard": True` in that method.

Dependencies for Python 3 and PyPy support
issue_body:
pthon-u2flib-host currently only Python 2.x (in particular CPython 2.x). The limiting factor is the required libraries. Porting python-u2flib-host would first require porting, or replacing
 - [M2Crypto](https://github.com/martinpaljak/M2Crypto)
 - [python-hidapi(https://github.com/trezor/cython-hidapi)

I'd like to propose M2Crypto be replaced, the project appears to be stalled (with no commits since May 2015) and it's written using SWIG which [isn't compatible with PyPy](https://bitbucket.org/pypy/compatibility/wiki/Home#!swig-swig)(https://bitbucket.org/pypy/compatibility/wiki/Home#!swig-swig). I'd like to propose [cryptography](https://github.com/pyca/cryptography)
 - Apache/BSD dual licensed
 - Supports Python 2.6-2.7, Python 3.3+ and PyPy
 - Tested on Windows, Mac OSX and Linux
 - Ships pre-built wheels for Python 32-bit and 64-bit on Windows
 - Has multiple maintainers i.e. a bus factor > 1

I'm happy to do the work and provide a PR, but first I'd like to check you're happy with the concept. Would Yubico be willing to use [cryptography] instead of M2Crypto for python-u2flib-host?

apdu_data = struct.pack('B B B B B B B %is B B' % size,0, ins, p1, p2, l0, l1, l2, data, 0x04, 0x00)
It shows that struct.error: required argument is not an integer

python-u2flib-server uses pyasn1 for a single purpose - to implement `get_ext_by_oid()`.
The recent release of cryptography 1.2 fixes pyca/cryptography#2288 this can be achieved with `Certificate.extensions.get_extension_for_oid()`.

However, `get_ext_by_oid()` and `ExtensionMatcher` are implemented around comparing the raw bytes of the extension and cryptography only exposes these when the oid is of an `UnrecognizedExtension`. In general the cryptography ethos strongly discourages exposing raw bytes.

Should we take this opportunity to change the return type of `get_ext_by_oid()`?

issue_title: tchannel-python assumes any incoming connections with host:port in init headers can be used to send RPCs
issue_body:
When TChannel receives an incoming connection, it receives init headers which include a host:port that represents whether the other side is listening.

tchannel-python currently assumes that any connection which has a host:port is OK to send RPCs back on. It previously also sent RPCs to connections that were marked ephemeral, but [that was fixed](https://github.com/uber/tchannel-python/pull/449).

If any service connects to a tchannel-python service, RPCs may end up going back over that incoming connection, and typically the client receives a `BadRequest` error (because the remote host:port was not expecting those RPCs). These requests are not retried, and cause an immediate failure.

issue_title: WordCloud doesn't clear itself when the data is removed
issue_body:
<!--
This is an issue template. Please fill in the relevant details in the
sections below.
-->

##### Text version
<!-- From menu _Options→Add-ons→Orange3-Text_ or code `orangecontrib.text.version.full_version` -->
master


##### Orange version
<!-- From menu _Help→About→Version_ or code `Orange.version.full_version` -->
master


##### Expected behavior
When one removes the data. the WordCloud should become empty.


##### Actual behavior
Last wordcloud is shown. 


##### Steps to reproduce the behavior
Pass a data to wordcloud, remove the data and observe wordcloud.


##### Additional info (worksheets, data, screenshots, ...)
![screen shot 2017-05-18 at 17 04 44](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/713026/26209082/1cc8348a-3bec-11e7-8bcd-b4119ca01e26.png)

In `Network.get_next_network()`, we currently do what @jathanism has coined as a "loose" allocation. For example:
1. 10.1.2.0/24 is in the DB as allocated
1. 10.1.2.0/25 is also in the DB as allocated
1. When `get_next_network()` is called on 10.1.2.0/24 (the parent) for a prefix length of /32, an address within 10.1.2.0/25 is offered

However, there's a use case where we'd want to request a "strict" allocation, meaning that 10.1.2.0/25 is considered off-limits and the above call to `get_next_network()` would instead consider an address from 10.1.2.128/25 since it doesn't exist in the database.

Django.template.loaders.filesystem.Loader: /app/microsoft_startups/templates/sortedm2m/sorted_checkbox_select_multiple_widget.html (Source does not exist)

infomation version:
Djnago: 1.10.1
Django-cms: 3.5+
Django-cms-blog: 0.9.2

issue_title: Add flag to mark page as noindex
Django-rest framework 'module' object is not callable
issue_body:
I have a model like this 
class ProductCategory(models.Model):
    name = models.CharField(max_length=150, primary_key = True)
    description = models.TextField()

    audit_log = AuditLog()

my serializer:

class ProductCategorySerializer(serializers.ModelSerializer):
    class Meta:
        model = ProductCategory

I got this error 
TypeError at /api/productcategoty/
'module' object is not callable

Do you know how to implement AuditLog() to work with rest framework?
Thank you
An

issue_title: configRemoteFeedbackFilter throws NotImplementedError. Is there a quick fix or workaround for this?
issue_body:
I'm trying to do Motion Profiling with 4 Talon_SRX's, 2 quadrature encoders and a pigeon_imu.
encoder 1 is plugged into talon_srx_1
encoder 2 is plugged into talon_srx_2
pigeon_imu is plugged into talon_srx_3
and talons 2, 3, 4 are set to follow talon one.

I'm trying to use only talon_1 for it's PID loop for encoders and aux PID loop for the pigeon.

Pretty much following this example from the java code: https://github.com/CrossTheRoadElec/Phoenix-Examples-Languages/blob/master/Java/RemoteClosedLoop/src/org/usfirst/frc/team4130/robot/Robot.java

But it seems like this function is not fully implemented yet in the python api. Is there a quick fix or workaround for this?
With all the subclassing wpilib has, it can be very useful to see the inherited members of a class. I think we should add this feature of showing all the functions a class has.

issue_title: Provide better third party hook support for HAL
issue_body: This will be necessary as third party drivers will be moving out of WPILib.
Perhaps add some examples?

issue_title: pipenv only attempts to resolve dependencies using the latest version

Hi All!

When running `pipenv lock` on a Pipfile containing `pandas==0.18.1` and `pandas-datareader==0.5.0`, `pipenv` errors out claiming that it can't find a pandas version satisfying `pandas==0.18.1,>=0.19.2`, but pandas-datareader doesn't require `pandas>=0.19.2` until `0.6.0`. issue_title: Recent master breaks dev build for pipenv
issue_body:
Short version: 9f41427 ("pip hash mode compatibility for pipenv lock -r") and later causes `pipenv install` with this repo's Pipfile to fail.  I tried to dig into why the (seemingly unrelated) change would cause this, but couldn't quite grok why this happened. (`v9.1.0` commit builds just fine)

##### Describe your environment

1. OS Type: OS X 10.12.6 (also fails in docker)
1. Python version: `Python 2.7.14`
1. Pipenv version: `$ pipenv --version`

##### Expected result

Expected pipenv to install

##### Actual result

This traceback (added a `print(package_name)` just to make debugging a little easier, whichi s why `-e .` dumps):

Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/local/bin/pipenv", line 11, in <module>
    load_entry_point('pipenv', 'console_scripts', 'pipenv')()
  File "/Users/jtratner/pipenv/pipenv/vendor/click/core.py", line 722, in __call__
    return self.main(*args, **kwargs)
  File "/Users/jtratner/pipenv/pipenv/vendor/click/core.py", line 697, in main
    rv = self.invoke(ctx)
  File "/Users/jtratner/pipenv/pipenv/vendor/click/core.py", line 1066, in invoke
    return _process_result(sub_ctx.command.invoke(sub_ctx))
  File "/Users/jtratner/pipenv/pipenv/vendor/click/core.py", line 895, in invoke
    return ctx.invoke(self.callback, **ctx.params)
  File "/Users/jtratner/pipenv/pipenv/vendor/click/core.py", line 535, in invoke

  File "/Users/jtratner/pipenv/pipenv/cli.py", line 207, in install
    deploy=deploy
  File "/Users/jtratner/pipenv/pipenv/core.py", line 1708, in do_install
    do_init(dev=dev, allow_global=system, ignore_pipfile=ignore_pipfile, system=system, skip_lock=skip_lock, verbose=verbose, concurrent=concurrent, deploy=deploy, pre=pre)
  File "/Users/jtratner/pipenv/pipenv/core.py", line 1321, in do_init
    skip_lock=skip_lock, verbose=verbose, concurrent=concurrent)
  File "/Users/jtratner/pipenv/pipenv/core.py", line 834, in do_install_dependencies
    index=index

    req = get_requirement(package_name.split('--hash')[0].split('--trusted-host')[0]).vcs

##### Steps to replicate

Put this in a script, it will fail
```
rm -r ~/pipenv-env || true
pip install virtualenv
virtualenv --python=python2.7 ~/pipenv-env
source ~/pipenv-env/bin/activate
python setup.py develop
pipenv install
```

For good measure, I confirmed this also fails in Docker:

```
FROM python:3.6.4

ADD . /repo
RUN cd /repo && bash ./script.sh
```

issue_body:
I'm on Debian Stretch, using `rxvt-unicode` as my terminal.

In certain situations, the first character of a line gets displaced - it ends up at the far right of my terminal, on the previous line. Look at the right edge of this screenshot:

![2018-03-07-110130_958x1059_scrot](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/163093/37086080-fb7793e2-21f6-11e8-87c5-b036bc398414.png)

issue_title: File requirement is invalid on Linux
issue_body:
This is almost the same as #2256 except the workaround (use forward slashes) doesn't work.
Pipenv 2018.05.18

This dependency works great on Windows

    python-ldap = {platform_system = "== 'Windows'", file = "./python_ldap-3.1.0-cp36-cp36m-win_amd64.whl"}

However, running `pipenv install --skiplock --dev` on Linux (the Docker image python:3.6 to be exact) gives this error:

> Installing dependencies from Pipfile…
> WARNING: Invalid requirement, parse error at "'./python'"
> ABORTING INSTALL... You will have to reinstall any packages that failed to install.

Is it because the file doesn't exist? I would expect it to not care because the marker doesn't match.

issue_title: Incorrect warning during the dependency resolution
issue_body:
When trying to `pipenv install populus` I get the following warning towards the end

```
Warning: Your dependencies could not be resolved. You likely have a mismatch in your sub-dependencies.
  You can use $ pipenv install --skip-lock to bypass this mechanism, then run $ pipenv graph to inspect the situation.
Could not find a version that matches eth-keys<0.2.0,>=0.1.0b3
Tried: 0.1.0b1, 0.1.0b1, 0.1.0b2, 0.1.0b2, 0.1.0b3, 0.1.0b3, 0.1.0b4, 0.1.0b4, 0.2.0b1, 0.2.0b1, 0.2.0b2, 0.2.0b2, 0.2.0b3, 0.2.0b3
```

As instructed I did `pipenv install --skip-lock populus` followed by `pipenv graph`

The last command (`pipenv graph`) listed the following (among other details)

    eth-keys [required: >=0.1.0b3,<0.2.0, installed: 0.1.0b4]

So it seems the install did go correctly (since it installed correct version of `eth-keys`)

Then why the warning ?

##### Describe your environment

1. OS Type : macOS Sierra
1. Python version: Python 3.6.2
1. Pipenv version: pipenv, version 11.0.2

##### Expected result

There should be no warning since it installed correct version.

##### Actual result
```
Warning: Your dependencies could not be resolved. You likely have a mismatch in your sub-dependencies.
  You can use $ pipenv install --skip-lock to bypass this mechanism, then run $ pipenv graph to inspect the situation.

Tried: 0.1.0b1, 0.1.0b1, 0.1.0b2, 0.1.0b2, 0.1.0b3, 0.1.0b3, 0.1.0b4, 0.1.0b4, 0.2.0b1, 0.2.0b1, 0.2.0b2, 0.2.0b2, 0.2.0b3, 0.2.0b3
```
##### Steps to replicate

    pipenv install populus

issue_title: specifying relative path as source for package

issue_body: https://pipfile.pypa.io/ give me a `DNS address could not be found` error.

Currently we find ourselves needing to patch the `setup.py` file of `setuptools` as it has `extras_require` and `dependency_links`, which don't agree with our package manager. We can provide these during the build. However, we find that `setuptools` tries to download them anyways. It would be nice if we had an option to disable these settings during the install or at least recognize that they are already accounted for.

cc @msarahan @pelson

issue_title: import pkg_resources.py31compat causes no module error
issue_body:
While pip installing jupyter with a [version of setuptools cloned just before this post](https://github.com/pypa/setuptools/tree/f443d77addf998ed760523faf76111686cc10367), I came across a no module error coming from setuptools:

`ImportError: No module named py31compat`

which comes from setuptools/setuptools/sandbox.py, line 15

`import pkg_resources.py31compat`

However, in `setuptools/pkg_resources/__init__.py`, line 72, py31compat is imported there:

`from . import py31compat`

Therefore, it seems unnecessary to do

`import pkg_resources.py31compat`

in sandbox.py. It's a python file already included when importing the pkg_resources submodule. One can just do:

`import pkg_resources`

This fixed the problem for me. 

Any reason for using an import statement directly to the python file within the submodule that already includes it in init.py?

I've also had this problem on older python environments while installing other libraries. Not sure what would change in an existing environment to cause this problem.

issue_title: No failure or warning for home_dir paths which break fragile shebang parsing
issue_body:
Virtualenv uses shebang parsing of the home_dir path. That is, it creates files (e.g. `bin/pip`) which begin with:

    #!"/path/to/home/dir/bin/python3.6"

The  `#!` is known as a shebang. The path after the shebang is parsed by the kernel on Linux and Mac OS X (though not on Windows). This kernel parsing is fragile: it can't tolerate 
*  whitespace in the path: see issue #53 "Whitespace in root path of virtualenv breaks scripts", #994 "Pip fails with space in virtualenv path"
* a path which is too long: #997 "Long env path names cause weird errors after activiting virtual environment"
* a path with emoji: see issue #1014 "Not compatible with a directory having emojis in its path"

All those issues propose various fixes. Some of the fixes involve proposing changes upstream to distutil or pip. The upstream teams are resisting some changes because they are OS-specific or fragile. It is taking time to work that out.

In the meantime I think virtualenv should check the home_dir path for any of these flaws. On Mac OS and Linux, it should fail with an error if a path has any of these problems. Maybe there should be a way to override the error if a user explicitly asks to. 

This issue is for the adding of a warning message to virtualenv, to reduce the impact of fragile shebang parsing until the above underlying issues are fixed, somehow.

It seems to me that adding this warning message should be a logically simple fix to [`virtualenv/virtualenv.py`](https://github.com/pypa/virtualenv/blob/master/virtualenv.py). Around line [685:688](https://github.com/pypa/virtualenv/blob/master/virtualenv.py#L685-L688), next to the check for `os.path.exists(home_dir)`, add a similar check for `is_valid(home_dir)`. Function `is_valid()` checks for all the fragilities exposed by the above issues.

The warning message could alternatively be a fatal error message. If so, then having an option like `-f` to override the error might also be a good addition.

issue_body:
Maybe related to https://github.com/requests/requests/issues/2422,
Sometimes, but quite frequently, we receive following response after a Minio.fput_object call:
```sh
WARNING Retrying (Retry(total=4, connect=None, read=None, redirect=None)) after connection broken by 'ProtocolError('Connection aborted.', BrokenPipeError(32, 'Broken pipe'))': /<bucket_id>/<file_id>?partNumber=1&uploadId=0fa52676-2bc7-45a1-a9fc-d878132b53e4
```
It then attempts to open a new HTTP connection but hangs and the only way to proceed is to kill the python process and restart it, after which it usually uploads fine.

Have any of you experienced this and is there a way to capture this error better so we can retry explicitly?

Here is the code snippet which causes this issue:
```python
def upload_to_bucket(self, bucket, file):
        logging.info("Sending file %s.", file)
        path = self.path_constructor.to_file_in_dataset(bucket, file)
        try:
            etag = self.minio_client.fput_object(bucket, file, path)
            logging.info("File %s uploader to MinIO. Received etag: %s", file, etag)
            return True
        except ResponseError as err:
            logging.error("Response error when uploading file (skipping): %s", err)
            return False
```
The warning message I get has to come from within Minio or the underlying urllib3, because our logger does not register successful or failed upload, the process hangs somewhere in the `fput_object`  call.

issue_title: Compiled regular expressions cache is exhausted by language detection
issue_body:
The `re` module's cache of compiled regular expressions is exhausted by the language detection when it fails to detect a language, causing the same regular expressions to be compiled again each time an invalid date is passed.
```
>>> import timeit
>>> from dateparser import DateDataParser
>>> ddp = DateDataParser(allow_redetect_language=True)
>>> timeit.timeit(lambda: ddp.get_date_data("not really a date"), number=100)
13.68727993965149
```

Increasing `re._MAXCACHE` prevents the cache from being cleared and all the patterns from being compiled again on each attempt.
```
>>> import timeit
>>> from dateparser import DateDataParser
>>> ddp = DateDataParser(allow_redetect_language=True)
>>> import re
>>> re._MAXCACHE = float('inf')
>>> timeit.timeit(lambda: ddp.get_date_data("not really a date"), number=100)
3.077785015106201
```

Profiling shows that most time is spent in `re.sub()` called by `Language._simplify()` and `re.compile()` called by `Dictionary._construct_split_regex()`.

issue_title: API calls being dropped due to improper queue.empty() call
issue_body:
Possible root cause of https://github.com/segmentio/analytics-python/issues/96

We were batching API calls (~5000 per day) to .identify() but were seeing 80% losses. The issue is that you are calling queue.empty() after the previous member was pulled from the queue, but before the next member was added. This resulted in a batch size greater than the default upload size (100), which resulted in totally rejected HTTP requests.

issue_title: Problem with uploading binary files with Python 3
issue_body:
With Python 3, uploading a binary file will fail:

```python
with open('/path/to/a/file.jpg', 'r') as fp:
    file_upload = stripe.FileUpload.create(
        purpose='dispute_evidence',
        file=fp
    )
```
fails with:
```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  <snip>
  File "/Users/ob/.pyenv/versions/3.5.1/lib/python3.5/site-packages/stripe/multipart_data_generator.py", line 28, in add_params
    self._write_file(value)
  File "/Users/ob/.pyenv/versions/3.5.1/lib/python3.5/site-packages/stripe/multipart_data_generator.py", line 54, in _write_file
    file_contents = f.read(self.chunk_size)
  File "/Users/ob/.pyenv/versions/3.5.1/lib/python3.5/codecs.py", line 321, in decode
    (result, consumed) = self._buffer_decode(data, self.errors, final)
UnicodeDecodeError: 'utf-8' codec can't decode byte 0x89 in position 0: invalid start byte
```

Opening the file as binary (e.g. replacing `'r'` by `'rb'` yields a different issue:
```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  <snip>
  File "/Users/ob/.pyenv/versions/3.5.1/lib/python3.5/site-packages/stripe/multipart_data_generator.py", line 28, in add_params
    self._write_file(value)
  File "/Users/ob/.pyenv/versions/3.5.1/lib/python3.5/site-packages/stripe/multipart_data_generator.py", line 57, in _write_file
    self._write(file_contents)
  File "/Users/ob/.pyenv/versions/3.5.1/lib/python3.5/site-packages/stripe/multipart_data_generator.py", line 50, in _write
    self.data.write(bytes(value, 'utf-8'))
TypeError: encoding or errors without a string argument
```

I think we ought to:

1. Update the Python file upload examples to use `mode='rb'` rather than `mode='r'` (since image files are binary files and it makes no sense to apply an encoding to them).

2. Patch [this method](https://github.com/stripe/stripe-python/blob/master/stripe/multipart_data_generator.py#L46-L50) that assumes that Python 3 will always deal with Unicode strings. Something like this should work:
```python
def _write(self, value):
  if sys.version_info < (3, 0) or isinstance(value, bytes):
    self.data.write(value)
  else:
    self.data.write(bytes(value, 'utf-8'))
```

@brandur, any thoughts? Should I proceed with the above?

Hi,

I am having some problems with very large workflows due to the modifications in the refresh method in the Workflow class made some time ago (https://github.com/materialsproject/fireworks/commit/854816f1ab22da671ce64c5201dac5a1238c7508).
After the introduction of the defuse_workflow action the refresh is called on all the updated_ids and not only on the direct children, but consider this example workflow: one starting FW, with many children FWs depending on it (so that the dependencies are given by {fw1: [fw2, ...,fw100]}).
After the completion of fw1,  the FWs fw2-100 should be refreshed. However, when fw2 is refreshed this will still require to refresh again all the fw1-fw100 as well and so on. The refresh function is then called recursively several times. This leads in the best case to a considerable slow down of the refresh and potentially to a crash if the number of FWs is large enough (RuntimeError: maximum recursion depth exceeded. On my system this happens when the number of FWs is around 1000).
I suggest to change the method like this: https://github.com/gpetretto/fireworks/commit/4ac12b15307410477623a29dcd77e90e45542937
but, as I am not sure about the possible problematic cases related to the defuse_workflow option, could you please check if this modification will still properly cover all the other cases?

Thanks,

Guido

The build process used to have requirements scattered across many scripts in travis. 
This was probably due to the fact that PyPi didn't have wheels and thus some optimization was done around that.
There seems to have been an attempt at cleaning this up a while back, but the build process is still needlessly complicated.

Many of these are small changes to files, but combined, it can be confusing to understand what happens. I'll try to split them up as different PRs.

 * [ ] 0. Ensure that Travis actually fails when the test script fails. 
    -  [ ] Master  #3170. 
    -  [ ] 0.14.x #3169 

**Everything below needs to be rebased onto 0. to ensure that a new build is actually triggered.**
 * [ ] 1. Remove stray pip commands in travis install that don't use a requirements file.
 * [ ] 2. Pin all requirements files and not just default.txt
 * [ ] 3. Remove the wheelhouse: BIG CHANGE!
    * Unfortunately, I think 1 and 2, 3 need to be done together.
    * Ensure that 
        * [ ] We install only build depdencies before the build.
        * [ ] Ensure that MINIMUM_REQUIREMENTS are actually pinned 
 * [ ] 4. Move the cythonize script outside of the `skimage` directory. PR #3158.
 * [ ] 5. Use `pip install .` to install the package as opposed to calling the setup.py script directly.
    * Since we don't have a wheelhouse anymore, we can ask pip to install all the dependencies listed in the `setup.py` file itself.
    * [ ] Ensure that the versions are correct in the minimum build number.
    * Will have to use `pytest --pyargs skimage` as opposed to `pytest skimage` since scikit-image isn't installed in development mode anymore.
 * [ ] 6. Consistent Cython versioning: should all require `0.23.4`: PR #3171
 * [ ] 7. Remove the tools/build_versions.sh script. This functionality is provided by `pip list` and breaks things as we add or remove dependencies.
 * [ ] 8. Remove matplotlib as a core dependency: WIP #3129
    - [ ] Matplotlib should be made into an optional dependency.
    - [ ] Matplotlib should be made into 
 * [ ] 9. Prefer imageio over PIL (though PIL is still a dependency for tiffle): WIP #3126
 * [ ] 10. Reduce the verbosity on the builds as travis is having a hard time keeping up.
    * [ ] Decrease Python messages
    * [ ] run `pytest` without `-v`

Many of these fixes are in #3159, but that PR got too big to handle.

Hi all,

In developing one of the advanced tutorials, I think I may have uncovered an error in our morphological opening operation. Here's the code:

```python

                                selem, remove_small_objects)
from scipy import ndimage as nd
strel = selem.disk(radius=2)

chrs = []
titles = []

chr0 = nd.binary_opening(chromosomes_adapt, strel)
chr1 = nd.binary_opening(chr0, strel)
chrs.append(remove_small_objects(chr1, 256))
titles.append("ndimage manual")

chr1 = nd.binary_opening(chromosomes_adapt, strel, iterations=2)
chrs.append(remove_small_objects(chr1, 256))
titles.append("ndimage iterations")

chr0 = opening(chromosomes_adapt, strel)
chr1 = opening(chr0, strel)
chrs.append(remove_small_objects(chr1, 256))
titles.append("skimage opening")

chrs.append(remove_small_objects(chr1, 256))
titles.append("skimage binary_opening")

issue_title: accounts: user creation with id = 1 problem on PostgreSQL
issue_body:
PostgreSQL doesn't update the sequence after insertion of [users fixtures](https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio/blob/master/invenio/modules/accounts/fixtures.py#L32) (user admin).

http://stackoverflow.com/questions/9108833/postgres-autoincrement-not-updated-on-explicit-id-inserts

Here you can read that:

> That's how it's supposed to work - next_val('test_id_seq') is only called when the system needs a value for this column and you have not provided one. If you provide value no such call is performed and consequently the sequence is not "updated".

So, after inserting the user admin in the table, the sequence associated with the id is not updated.
It means that next user that will be inserted will generate the error:

```python
IntegrityError: (psycopg2.IntegrityError) duplicate key value violates unique constraint "user_pkey"
DETAIL:  Key (id)=(1) already exists.
```

A possible solution is to not specify the id in `fixtures.py`.
Any ideas? :)

The default site URL used in the developer setup is `0.0.0.0`. Recently, Chrome is not working so well with this URL prefix (http://stackoverflow.com/questions/28112933/how-to-stop-chrome-from-searching-when-manually-typing-in-local-dev-addresses). 

Even the [wikipedia entry](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/0.0.0.0) for this URL states:
> 0.0.0.0 is a non-routable meta-address used to designate an invalid, unknown or non applicable target.

It's recommended to use `127.0.0.1` or `localhost` instead. Shall we change this default everywhere in the docs and elsewhere applicable?

issue_body:
[Schema 4.0 documentation](https://schema.labs.datacite.org/meta/kernel-4.0/)

Changes:
- [ ] Allowing more than one nameIdentifier per creator or contributor
- [ ] Addition of new optional subproperties for creatorName and contributorName
  - familyName
  - givenName
- [ ] Addition of new titleType “Other”
- [ ] Addition of new subproperty for subjectScheme
  - subjectScheme
    - valueURI
- [ ] Changing resourceTypeGeneral from optional to mandatory
- [ ] Addition of a new relatedIdentifierType option “IGSN”
- [ ] Addition of a new descriptionType "TechnicalInfo"
- [ ] Addition of a new subproperty for GeoLocation “geoLocationPolygon”
- [ ] Changing the definition of the existing GeoLocation sub properties (geoLocationPoint, and geoLocationBox)
- [ ] Addition of a new property: FundingReference, with subproperties
  - funderName
  - funderIdentifier 
    - funderIdentifierType 
  - awardNumber
  - awardURI
  - awardTitle
- [ ] Deprecation of contributorType “funder” (as a result of adding the new property “FundingReference”)

Implementation:
- [ ] Add `datacite/schema40.py` modeled over `datacite/schema31.py`.
- [ ] Ensure common definition of identical rules for v3.1/v4.0.
- [ ] Validator for v4.0
- [ ] Implement new/changed rules.
- [ ] Add tests.
- [ ] Update documentation example to use v4.0
- [ ] Add JSON Schema `schemas/datacite-v4.0.json`

issue_title: app: Use provided HTTPException description in error handlers

issue_title: views: define signals for view methods
issue_body:
At the moment, there is no official way to perform any actions before or after a record REST API request (or to be more precise, after a record CRUD operation has been committed to the database). 

Sending signals at the end of each REST resource method would be a solution to this problem, and allow for such pluggable actions. The end result would be something like `after_records_post`, `after_record_put`, `after_record_delete`, etc.

### The ugly part

Although this might be frowned upon since it's introducing the danger of a user of the module modifying the database state or creating side effects, it's a logical use case for when there is a need for sequential execution of actions. A common example is record indexing, where a created/modified/deleted record should be sent for the appropriate indexing operation on ElasticSearch, in order to appear or be removed from the search results. This would prevent the following pattern from happening:

1. `POST /api/records`
    - DB transaction started
        - Payload validated
        - Record with PID `recid:1` created in transaction
        - Record sent for indexing
    - DB commit **fails**
2. `GET /api/records`
    - Record with PID `recid:1` available in results
3. `GET /api/records/1`
    - `404 Not found`

*(Another issue is the fact that signals have proven to be hard to debug, especially in complex operations (eg. deposit actions), so introducing more of them won't make things easier... This though is general issue that might need better handling on a global level, such as better documentation of signals or keeping a programmatically accessible registry of signals for debugging purposes)*

Currently the size of the tree (`__len__`) is calculated on demand and involves loading and querying all buckets. I think it would be reasonable to track the number of items explicitly (by counting through insertions and deletions), so that querying it doesn't need to load anything except the root BTree object.

Naturally this changes the pickle layout, so it would require extra compat code and would not be backwards compatible.

Perhaps something for a major release?

issue_title: Properly reading results from Hive queries in Pandas in Python 3
issue_body:
What is the best way to read the output from disk with Pandas after using `cmd.get_results` ? (e.g. from a Hive command).

For example, consider the following:
``` python

out_file = 'results.csv'
delimiter = chr(1)
....

Qubole.configure(qubole_key)
hc_params = ['--query', query]
hive_args = HiveCommand.parse(hc_params)
cmd = HiveCommand.run(**hive_args)
if (HiveCommand.is_success(cmd.status)):
  with open(out_file, 'wt') as writer:
  cmd.get_results(writer, delim=delimiter, inline=False)
```

If, after successfully running the query, I then inspect the first few bytes of `results.csv`, I see the following:
```
$ head -c 300 results.csv
b'flight_uid\twinning_price\tbid_price\timpressions_source_timestamp\n'b'0FY6ZsrnMy\x012000\x012270.0\x011427243278000\n0FamrXG9AW\x01710\x01747.0\x011427243733000\n0FY6ZsrnMy\x012000\x012270.0\x011427245266000\n0FY6ZsrnMy\x012000\x012270.0\x011427245088000\n0FamrXG9AW\x01330\x01747.0\x011427243407000\n0FamrXG9AW\x01710\x01747.0\x011427243981000\n0FamrXG9AW\x01490\x01747.0\x011427245289000\n
```

When I try to open this in [Pandas](http://pandas.pydata.org/):

`df = pd.read_csv('results.csv')`

it obviously doesn't work (I get an empty DataFrame), since it isn't properly formatted as a csv file. 

While I could try to open `results.csv` and post-process it (to remove `b'`, etc.) before I open it in Pandas, this would be a quite hacky way to load it.

Am I using the interface correctly? This is using the very last version of `qds_sdk`: `1.4.2` from a three hours ago.

I'd like to suggest we use Pandas implementation for rolling regressions. 
https://github.com/pydata/pandas/blob/master/pandas/stats/ols.py

The performance difference is huge.

For a single factor
* rolling_beta: 1 loops, best of 3: 4.26 s per loop
* pd.ols: 1 loops, best of 3: 275 ms per loop

For multiple factors
* rolling_multifactor_beta: 1 loops, best of 3: 15.2 s per loop
* pd.ols: 1 loops, best of 3: 265 ms per loop

Pandas uses some sophisticated caching methods that make their implementation really fast. I'm slightly suspect of the differences between the results of the two implementations as well, I know the pandas method takes takes takes float precision into account.

If there are no arguments against this I can swap it out and submit a PR.

issue_title: backwards compat: empyrical.cum_returns_final used to support dataframes
issue_body:
https://github.com/quantopian/bayesalpha/commit/c57713fb7db2fd5e10804d1e1e74ee543ddd3dbd broke a (non-explicit and apparently untested) use-case where a user can pass in a DataFrame with multiple columns and get the cumulative returns for each column. After this change, it always returns a scalar. This quietly breaks user-code.

Ideally we revert it to the old way, or at least raise an error that dataframe inputs are not supported.

CC @llllllllll

this line in the serializer causes turtle output to lose datatype info
https://github.com/RDFLib/rdflib/blob/master/rdflib/plugins/serializers/turtle.py#L304

```
from rdflib.term import Literal
from rdflib.namespace import XSD
print(Literal(10, datatype=XSD.decimal)._literal_n3(use_plain=True))
print(Literal(10, datatype=XSD.decimal)._literal_n3(use_plain=False))
```
results in:
```
10.0
"10"^^<http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#decimal>
```

in the first scenario the round trip loses the info that the value is a decimal with no '.0', simply '10'

issue_title: Discrepancy between h5 and neurolicuda ASCII data
issue_body:
Related to #322. 

Two files supposedly representing the same neurons in different formats show small differences in some of the morphometrics obtained with `neurom`.

It is not clear whether this is a problem with the data or an artifact of `neurom`'s data readers, so it might require some detailed investigation of the data itself, and / or the production of `h5` and `neurolucida` samples that we can be completely sure are equivalent. 

This is an example highlighting the issue:

```python
h5 number of sections: 562.0
nl number of sections: 563.0

h5 number of segments: 5659.0
nl number of segments: 6216.0

h5 total neurite surface area: 22321.4425649
nl total neurite surface area: 22186.5760705

h5 total neurite volume: 2533.15174575
nl total neurite volume: 2423.22948318

h5 total neurite length: 21075.2318211
nl total neurite length: 21077.0057435
```

This is the code I used:

```python
import numpy as np
from neurom import ezy

nrn_h5 = ezy.load_neuron('C050600B1.h5')
nrn_asc = ezy.load_neuron('C050600B1.asc')

print 'h5 number of sections: %s' % ezy.get('number_of_sections', nrn_h5)[0]
print 'nl number of sections: %s\n' % ezy.get('number_of_sections', nrn_asc)[0]
print 'h5 number of segments: %s' % ezy.get('number_of_segments', nrn_h5)[0]
print 'nl number of segments: %s\n' % ezy.get('number_of_segments', nrn_asc)[0]
print 'h5 total neurite surface area: %s' % np.sum(ezy.get('section_areas', nrn_h5))
print 'nl total neurite surface area: %s\n' % np.sum(ezy.get('section_areas', nrn_asc))
print 'h5 total neurite volume: %s' % np.sum(ezy.get('section_volumes', nrn_h5))
print 'nl total neurite volume: %s\n' % np.sum(ezy.get('section_volumes', nrn_asc))
print 'h5 total neurite length: %s' % np.sum(ezy.get('section_lengths', nrn_h5))
print 'nl total neurite length: %s\n' % np.sum(ezy.get('section_lengths', nrn_asc))
```

The sample files can be found [here](https://github.com/BlueBrain/NeuroM/files/248269/Morphs.zip)

issue_body: Relates to openfisca/openfisca-france#926 Supersedes openfisca/openfisca-france#1033 **Objective** Deduce ~10-20% the time taken when launching several tests at the same time. **Definition of done** - [ ] Extract type checking from app's life-cycle (including tests) - [ ] Execute type checking on demand (i.e. `make typecheck`) - [ ] Execute type checking on CircleCI **Details** From openfisca/openfisca-france#1014 we know type checking represents an important time of the test suite's run time: That's ~35% of total test suite's run time. **Useful links** - https://github.com/openfisca/openfisca-france/issues/1014#issuecomment-402970760 - https://github.com/openfisca/openfisca-core/issues/616#issuecomment-406545206 - https://github.com/openfisca/openfisca-france/files/2176976/monkeytype_call_traces.xlsx - https://github.com/python/mypy **Tips** There's no drop-in replacement of type-checking without refactoring, so it is advisable to use a _divide and conquer stratégie_ : - Pick one function to experiment with - Extract one type-checking to another function (obvious candidates: `isinstance`) - Try to refactor that one to see how difficult it is to gradually typecheck - Share - Desist or repeat

issue_title: Emulation exception handler is overly broad
issue_body:
### OS / Environment
Ubuntu 16.04

### Manticore version
`master`

### Python version
2.7

### Summary of the problem
Instruction emulation with Unicorn needs to concretize all registers, since Unicorn is a purely concrete emulator. When a register contains symbolic data, `emulate` raises `ConcretizeRegister` to obtain a solution. However, the [exception handler](https://github.com/trailofbits/manticore/blob/77b0542b/manticore/core/cpu/abstractcpu.py#L854) for Unicorn catches all exceptions and wraps them into `InstructionEmulationError`s.

### Step to reproduce the behavior
```
user@ubuntu /m/h/p/manticore> cat ret.c
#include <unistd.h>
int main(void) {
    int ret;
    read(0, &ret, sizeof ret);
    return ret;
}
user@ubuntu /m/h/p/manticore> gcc -static ret.c -o ret
user@ubuntu /m/h/p/manticore> manticore ret
2017-12-01 08:08:30,397: [8804] m.manticore:INFO: Loading program ret
2017-12-01 08:08:31,397: [8804] m.manticore:INFO: Starting 1 processes.
2017-12-01 08:08:35,585: [8804] m.c.executor:ERROR: Exception:
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/mnt/hgfs/projects/manticore/manticore/core/executor.py", line 460, in run
    if not current_state.execute():
  File "/mnt/hgfs/projects/manticore/manticore/core/state.py", line 126, in execute
    result = self._platform.execute()
  File "/mnt/hgfs/projects/manticore/manticore/platforms/linux.py", line 2007, in execute
    self.current.execute()
  File "/mnt/hgfs/projects/manticore/manticore/core/cpu/abstractcpu.py", line 820, in execute
    self.emulate(insn)
  File "/mnt/hgfs/projects/manticore/manticore/core/cpu/abstractcpu.py", line 857, in emulate
    raise InstructionEmulationError(str(e))
InstructionEmulationError```

In the mcore workspace, the generated `.txt` files, contain concretized symbolic inputs.

https://github.com/trailofbits/manticore/blob/77b0542b83bce9695aa79d7447aa58f5c056b111/manticore/core/workspace.py#L531-L534

An improved extension for this file would be `.inputs` which is more intuitive and clear.

this is a backwards incompatible interface change.

issue_title: Transactions are commited in CommitRequest.NON_TRANSACTIONAL mode
issue_body:
We have just been bitten by this using the latest `0.5.0`. 


We had the following piece of code, run in two different threads:
```python
with dataset.transaction as t:
  entity = dataset.get(entity_key)
  archived_entity = _move_to_archive_key(entity)
  t.delete(entity.key)
  t.put(archived_entity)
  return archived_entity
```

We've seen two copies of the `archived_entity` for the same `entity`.  We've managed to reproduce it with two threads performing this concurrently (running separate Connections each, because httplib2 is not thread safe).

Having used Datastore's underlying technology before, I expected to get a `ConcurrentModificationException` on delete, and one of the threads throwing it, similar to what the Java API for AppEngine describes:
https://cloud.google.com/appengine/docs/java/datastore/transactions#Java_Uses_for_transactions

However, both of them succeed creating two copies of `archived_entity`, and there's no `ConcurrentModificationException` anywhere in `gcloud-python` code.

We've tracked down to the following:

 * `Transaction#commit` calls the `Batch#commit` https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/gcloud-python/blob/master/gcloud/datastore/transaction.py#L177
 * `Batch#commit` is unaware of the `Transaction#id`, calls `Connection#commit` without the `transaction_id` parameter https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/gcloud-python/blob/master/gcloud/datastore/batch.py#L214
 * `Connection#commit`gets `transaction_id`, and sets the REST request's mode to `datastore_pb.CommitRequest.NON_TRANSACTIONAL` in https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/gcloud-python/blob/master/gcloud/datastore/connection.py#L321

*Impact*: This means that **all** transactions made through `python-gcloud` are **non-transactional**. Please treat it as a P0 bug.

StreamingPull currently has a "protocol" level bug affecting all languages

## Problem description
When streaming, `StreamingPullResponse` can be buffered, either by gRPC itself or by proxy/firewall.
There might be significant delay between the server sending a response and the client library reading it.
For this reason, by the time client library decides it should send a modify ack deadline ("modack"), it might be too late. The message might have already expired and resent.

Experimenting with Java client, we have seen many messages delivered 3-5 times because of this problem.

## What server-side is doing
To fix this, pubsub team is implementing the "receipt" feature.
The server will not start the expiration timer until it receives a receipt from the client [1]. A receipt here is similar to an ack. A receipt notifies the server that the client has *seen* the message, while an ack notifies that the client has finished with it.
This should make the client and server start expiration timer closer to each other.

[1] There's actually a limit on how long the server is willing to wait, so that a message isn't lost forever if the client doesn't send a receipt.

## What the client needs to do
The client should be modified to send these receipts.
For compatibility, these "receipts" are just modack requests. The client should modack messages after seeing it, in addition to what it is currently doing. These modacks can be batched for performance.

## Few more details for the curious
These modacks/receipts aren't strictly necessary for avoiding duplication, but it will help pubsub server expire messages on time. Without them, messages might expire too late.

Each `StreamingPullResponse` can contain multiple messages. In an implementation about to roll out, a modack for any message in the response will serve as receipts for all messages in that response. Still, client libs are encouraged to actually modack all messages since this behavior might change in the future.

issue_title: BigQuery: remove circular imports in helper functions that use Row class
issue_labels: [{'id': 82126549, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/api:%20bigquery', 'name': 'api: bigquery', 'color': 'cd7f32', 'default': False}, {'id': 544604243, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/priority:%20p2+', 'name': 'priority: p2+', 'color': 'ffee58', 'default': False}, {'id': 77438582, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/type:%20enhancement', 'name': 'type: enhancement', 'color': '8bc34a', 'default': False}]
issue_body:
With https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/pull/4291 the `Row` class was moved from `_helpers.py` to `table.py`. This caused circular references between the two files, because `_helpers.py` imports `Row` from `Table` and `table.py` imports multiple helper functions from `_helpers.py`.

To temporarily resolve the import errors, the `Row` class was imported within the individual helper functions where it was needed. The circular import issue needs to be resolved so that Row does not have to be imported inside functions.

issue_body:
As of @tseaver request, here's a new issue.

I've already opened a [issue](https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/python-docs-samples/issues/1364) in the python-docs-samples repository only later realizing this here would be a better place later.

To summarize the other issue:
`grpc._channel._Rendezvous` (the result of `SpeechClient.streaming_recognize`) is blocking if nothing can be transcribed (e.g. mic is muted, nothing is being spoken, etc.).

In an `asyncio` environment this is really bad.

What I was hoping for was something like:
```python
...
responses = client.streaming_recognize(config, requests)
while True:
    if not responses.has_result():
        continue

    response = next(responses)
```
This is just a quick draft but would allow proper integration into an `asyncio` environment as I control the loop.

I did try `grpc._channel._Rendezvous.result` with a `timeout` but I've only been getting `FutureTimeoutError` without any result (obviously I was speaking at this time).

I hope I got my issue across ':D

https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/compute-image-packages/blob/3d67bc6271650c8e1fc19b8420c17f6e204d1272/google_compute_engine_oslogin/pam_module/pam_oslogin_admin.cc#L89

Hello,

I reviewed this package as part of the Ubuntu Main Inclusion Review process, and found a few small items I think should be addressed.

If this file was created with more relaxed permissions, perhaps due to an unexpected `umask(2)` setting, a local attacker may be able to race the `chmod(2)` call. If C++ doesn't have a RAII method to set the modes at file creation time, please use `chmod(2)` manually to set the mode restrictively here.

Thanks

issue_title: dumping lambdas can give 2 different results
issue_body:
This is a strange issue I've encountered while using dill.

If the following code is put in a python file, the first run and all other runs will produce output which differ by 1 byte:

    dill.dumps(lambda: None)

This bug affects it's use to hash lambda arguments to function calls as it can be different between runs: https://github.com/joblib/joblib/pull/240

I've tracked this down to dill's `save_code` which calls `marshal.dumps` which produces the anolomous byte, but am not sure about the cause of this difference. I'm on python 3.5 windows 7 x64. Perhaps something other than marshal can be used, or it's output can be parsed to kill this difference?

issue_title: Pants should encode the minimally specific Hashbang into a PEX
issue_body:
# Issue:
I attempted to create a PEX for usage with a Python3 Interpreter (a procedure that lacks documentation in itself). I ended up creating a pants.ini with the following section

```
[python-setup]
interpreter_requirement: CPython>=3
```
This did successfully create PEX with a Python 3 Hashbang, however the Hashbang was unnecessarily specific, in this case the Hashbang was:

``` #!/usr/bin/env python3.5 ```

As expected this failed after transfer to a CentOS machine with only Python 3.4 present.
Technically and as specified in the requirement both Python versions would have been sufficient, however, due to the generated Hashbang, the PEX was limited to Python 3.5.

Most Python installations are likely to provide symlinks to allowing Hashbangs such as
```
 #!/usr/bin/env python
 #!/usr/bin/env python2
 #!/usr/bin/env python3
```
that would reflect the requirement specification given in pants.ini way better and would not enforce unnecessary restrictions on the portability / usability of the built PEX.

# Desired outcome:
- The generated Hashbang should be the least specific Python version based on the specification given in the pants.ini
- A section on selecting different Python versions should be introduced into the pants documentation. I might write the latter if provided with information on where to put it and if the way of using pants.ini for this is the desired way.

issue_title: Execute all CI shards in a uniform Docker container and make it easy to dev against it.
issue_body:
Ala dtox: https://github.com/pantsbuild/pex/issues/526
Relates to #2162

There is now a 2 AMI setup for our Amazon EC2 Jenkins plugin.  The cloud is capped at 11 instances, the prod AMI is capped at 10, leaving a canary to fill the gap.  Normally the canary AMI will == the prod AMI, but if AMI changes are needed, it can be altered to point to a new one.

Document all this and add some debug output to our Jenkinsfile node step to print out enough identifying information to easily figure out if a node may have failed due to a bad canary vs a normal CI failure.

Request from one of our developers:

With the most recent versions of javac, the `apt` step is obsolete. Javac can run the annotation processor in the same step, wouldn't it be nice if we didn't have to add extra configuration for it?  Maybe we could look into this as a part of the new engine refactoring.

issue_title: Experimentally expose the rust Scheduler to v1 Tasks and test the integration
issue_body:
The goal of this task is to experimentally expose the rust Scheduler to v1 Tasks so that they can directly/synchronously:
1. execute product requests (which can trigger things like #4397's process execution)
2. synchronously call into Snapshot APIs that will be implemented by #4585

Capturing Snapshots and executing isolated processes by poking the scheduler directly is sufficient for writing integration tests, and to allow for `compile` and `test` to be ported to remote process execution.

But this will be an *experimental/private API*, because it will not be possible to use it to cache (in memory in the rust Graph) any of these executions, since by the time v1 Tasks are running, we have already forked. #4769 covers the design for a longer term, cacheable integration between v1 Tasks and v2 products.

Issue Template is not present in the repository.

I want to work on this Issue.

issue_title: Set throttling and all of the other parameters supported by StageDescription
issue_body:
Hi @sanathkr,

Would it be possible to specify all of the parameters for an Api deployment, e.g. per-stage (or even per-method) ThrottlingRateLimit etc.? `AWS::ApiGateway::Deployment` already supports that, but it doesn't update Stage when re-deploying the Api. So if we benefit from automatic redeployments by `AWS::Serverless::Api`, we have to sacrifice control over throttling etc. Would it be possible to enable full functionality for the latter?

Thanks

issue_title: AWS::Lambda::Permission for log-group subscription filter fails to apply lambda:InvokeFunction action on CloudWatch logs
issue_body: Hi team, ## Description: I'm trying to automate my stack by doing: - Create Function - Create ApiGateway - Create LogGroup ------------------------> need AWS::Lambda::Permission here (for complete automation) - Create a subscription filter for the logGroup to stream the logs to a different lambda. ## Problem Statement: When I try the 4th step inside the template, the stack fails by saying: ## Template.yml: ``` AWSTemplateFormatVersion: 2010-09-09 Transform: AWS::Serverless-2016-10-31 Parameters: executionRole: Type: String Default: execution_role s3Bucket: Type: String Default: functions_bucket s3ObjectKey: Type: String functionName: Type: String Default: poc_lambda logCollectorLambdaName: Type: String Default: cloudwatch-log-collector-lambda stageName: Type: String Default: myTestStage aliasName: Type: String Default: test Resources: ApiGatewayApi: Type: AWS::Serverless::Api Properties: StageName: !Sub ${stageName} DefinitionBody: swagger: "2.0" info: title: !Sub ${functionName} paths: "/commerce": get: responses: {} x-amazon-apigateway-integration: uri: !Sub "arn:aws:apigateway:${AWS::Region}:lambda:path/2015-03-31/functions/arn:aws:lambda:${AWS::Region}:${AWS::AccountId}:function:${functionName}:${aliasName}/invocations" httpMethod: POST type: aws_proxy Commerce: Type: AWS::Serverless::Function Properties: FunctionName: !Ref functionName AutoPublishAlias: !Ref aliasName Description: "poc lambda" Handler: src/index.handler Runtime: nodejs6.10 CodeUri: Bucket: !Ref s3Bucket Key: !Ref s3ObjectKey Events: Catalog: Type: Api Properties: RestApiId: !Ref ApiGatewayApi Method: GET Path: /commerce MemorySize: 128 Timeout: 3 Role: !Sub "arn:aws:iam::${AWS::AccountId}:role/${executionRole}" Tags: name: !Ref functionName AutoPublishAlias: !Ref aliasName LogGroup: Type: AWS::Logs::LogGroup Properties: LogGroupName: !Sub "/aws/lambda/${functionName}" RetentionInDays: 14 LogGroupLambdaInvokePermission: Type: AWS::Lambda::Permission Properties: FunctionName: !Sub "arn:aws:lambda:${AWS::Region}:${AWS::AccountId}:function:${functionName}" Action: "lambda:InvokeFunction" Principal: !Sub "logs.${AWS::Region}.amazonaws.com" SourceArn: !Sub "arn:aws:logs:${AWS::Region}:${AWS::AccountId}:log-group:/aws/lambda/${functionName}:*" SourceAccount: Ref: "AWS::AccountId" DependsOn: "LogGroup" LogsSubscriptionFilter: Type: AWS::Logs::SubscriptionFilter Properties: LogGroupName: !Sub "/aws/lambda/${functionName}" FilterPattern: "" DestinationArn: !Sub "arn:aws:lambda:${AWS::Region}:${AWS::AccountId}:function:${logCollectorLambdaName}" DependsOn: "LogGroupLambdaInvokePermission" ``` ## Research: I read a lot of blogs that implement this design - 1. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonCloudWatch/latest/logs/SubscriptionFilters.html 2. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSCloudFormation/latest/UserGuide/aws-resource-lambda-permission.html#cfn-lambda-permission-sourceaccount _which is a subset of_ https://docs.aws.amazon.com/lambda/latest/dg/API_AddPermission.html#SSS-AddPermission-request-SourceArn 3. https://medium.com/@Prestomation/serverless-slackops-real-time-linking-to-logs-when-your-lambda-function-crashes-edbd8e0353e1 4. http://blog.davidablack.net/2016/08/17/a-log-reflector-for-aws-lambda/ 5. https://rockytech.blogspot.com/2017/02/massively-integrating-aws-cloudwatch.html went through some of the open/closed issues in SAM - - https://github.com/awslabs/serverless-application-model/issues/144, https://github.com/awslabs/serverless-application-model/issues/257 and few others (that almost talk about the issue). and also did more than 50 Cloudformation deployments with different combinations to make this work. ## Workaround: The only workaround for having the subscription filter in the template and still make this work, is to: 1. **Remove** the subscription filter on the first deployment. This will only create the logGroup (because that's in the template). 2. After this, you can add the subscription filter manually by navigating to Cloudwatch and putting the subscription on the logGroup. **AWS will create the AWS::Lambda::Permission for you**, which you cannot view (?). 3. Once the subscription filter is in place, you can add back the code in the template that creates the subscription filter. 4. On all the subsequent runs, *even if the stack is cleared out and recreated*, the subscription filter permission is intact with AWS and you don't get the error message anymore. In short, you won't need the `AWS::Lambda::Permission` in the template anymore, because AWS has that automatically created for you. ## It would be very helpful if anyone can point out the issue (if any) in the template, OR assure me that this is a bug, and I did not waste last 3 days over this problem. Thanks, Ravi Pandey.

issue_title: Change policy to prefer support questions on google group over github
issue_body:
Hallo everyone.

For a while now we have been lagging a little behind with the issues and that sometimes means that important bug reports don't get the priority they should have and that PR's are not reviewed for too long.

One issue that I see is that we get both bug reports, enhancements (PR's) and support request here on github. Of those three items, the first 2 needs developers, but the support questions can often be handled by committed users.

I therefore suggest to change the description in various places (home page, Github front page readme and PyPI) to say that support questions are **preferably** reported via the google group and, if the google group is not available to a user, then reported on github.

If we don't mind requiring that people join the google group, we could also say that support requests go exclusively to the google group, but I think that is maybe a bit too restrictive for my taste.

What do you think about it?

Regards Kenneth

issue_title: coach summary API doesn't count all items
issue_body:
## Summary

When looking at the summaries for channels with little or no existing activity, the summaries don't include accurate information about the contents of the channel.

In particular, the `progress` array only contains overviews for content types that have had at least one recorded user interaction with them. This means that (for example) the number of videos in the channel might be reported as `0` if none have been watched yet.


## Traceback or relevant snippet from server.log or browser console

content summary before anyone watched a video:

```javascript
{
  "pk": "4c8abbdb27f94d568d32c402f0dd0fc2",
  "content_id": "f63f0af81c894144b5e2c98c3f487089",
  "title": "Khan Academy (en) staging",
  "progress": [
    {
      "kind": "exercise",
      "node_count": 38,
      "total_progress": 0
    },
  ],
  "kind": "topic",
  "last_active": "2016-11-14T04:12:56.720Z",
  "ancestors": [],
  "num_users": 2
}
```

and after briefly watching part of a video:

```
{
  "pk": "4c8abbdb27f94d568d32c402f0dd0fc2",
  "content_id": "f63f0af81c894144b5e2c98c3f487089",
  "title": "Khan Academy (en) staging",
  "progress": [
    {
      "kind": "exercise",
      "node_count": 38,
      "total_progress": 0
    },
    {
      "kind": "video",
      "node_count": 59,
      "total_progress": 0
    }
  ],
  "kind": "topic",
  "last_active": "2016-11-14T04:12:56.720Z",
  "ancestors": [],
  "num_users": 2
}
```

## How to reproduce

Look at API results for a freshly imported channel.

## Real-life consequences (anything community should be aware of, for instance how it affects your deployment)

Coach reports are sometimes misleading.

### Observed behavior
Following various bugs or unknown errors, there is an appearance of a red box with json error. This format of displaying the error isn't particularly useful or informative to the user, and offers no information, it just breaks the current page the user is on. 

### Expected behavior
Some kind of friendly and informative way of conveying that an unexplainable error has occurred in Kolibri.

More informative and actionable error handling for unknown errors occurring in Kolibri. The more clarity we have in the design, the less put off users will be. We will also provide users with various options to report their error.

issue_title: Task.app_id is None when using c.get_app("xxx").tasks
issue_body:
When I call with `c.list_tasks(app_id)`, there's no problem.

But when I call with `c.get_app(app_id).tasks[0].app_id`, I found it's `None`.

After digging into the code a bit, I found it's caused by the marathon API `/v2/apps/<app_id>` and `/v2/apps/<app_id>/tasks` return different task format.

issue_title: libjuju fails on unknown facade versions
issue_body:
If libjuju is connected to a controller which supports a newer facade version than libjuju supports, it will fail instead of gracefully falling back to the maximum version that both it and the controller support (which is what the Juju client does).

We will also need to include a test which explicitly checks for any unknown facade versions so that can be run against upcoming Juju releases or PRs and inform us when new facades are available.

issue_title: removeGaps in Reads filtering does not make the corresponding change to the quality string
issue_body:
In `reads.py`:

```python
        if self.removeGaps:
            # TODO: There's a bug here. The sequence can be shortened but
            # the quality string (if any) is not.
            sequence = read.sequence.replace('-', '')
            read = read.__class__(read.id, sequence, read.quality)
```
Should instead be something like:
```python
        if self.removeGaps:
            if read.quality is not None:
                newSequence = []
                newQuality = []
                for base, q in zip(read.sequence, read.quality):
                    if base != '-':
                        newSequence.append(base)
                        newQuality.append(q)
                read = read.__class__(read.id, ''.join(newSequence), ''.join(newQuality)
            else:
                sequence = read.sequence.replace('-', '')
                read = read.__class__(read.id, sequence, read.quality)
```

issue_title: Problem with monitoring of outgoing updates
Invalid 'Rate-limit' and 'Value' in JSON output for extended communities
issue_body:
ISSUE TYPE
Bug Report

OS
Ubuntu 16.04.5 (Debian/Linux)

VERSION
```
ExaBGP : 4.0.2-1c737d99
Python : 3.5.2 (default, Nov 23 2017, 16:37:01)  [GCC 5.4.0 20160609]
Uname  : Linux exabgp01 4.4.0-101-generic #124-Ubuntu SMP Fri Nov 10 18:29:59 UTC 2017 x86_64
Root   : /opt/exabgp

```

ENVIRONMENT 
```
[exabgp.api]
encoder = text
respawn = false

[exabgp.daemon]
user = 'root'

[exabgp.log]
destination = '/var/log/exabgp.log'
level = DEBUG
routes = true


```

CONFIGURATION

```

process syslog {
        run /usr/bin/python /home/path/to/script.py;
        encoder json;
}


neighbor 192.168.194.100 {
        description "some-description";
        hold-time 180;
        listen 179;
        peer-as 65534;
        router-id 192.168.194.200;
        local-as 65534;
        local-address 192.168.194.200;
    api {
        processes [ syslog ];
        neighbor-changes;
        receive {
            parsed;
            update;
        }
    }

    }

```


SUMMARY
The exabgp is configured to receive messages from a BGP peer in the JSON format. For BGP Update (Flowspec) messages, the JSON contains key-value pair for the key - 'extended-community' containing values like 'rate-limit' and 'value'. Firstly, the rate-limit always shows as '0' in spite of the peer being configured to send a non-zero rate limit. Secondly, the 'value' which contains the decimal representation of the packet has a 'L' appended to end of the integer value. This creates issues when processing the JSON, since a value with a letter is seen as 'string' but results in an error since it does not have 'quotes' around it. For example: "value":  9225340944352543000L

The setup

```
import cytoolz.curried as cyt


def source(N=10):
    for n in range(N):
        yield n
    print('source: After all is done')
        
def foo(i):
    for n in i:
        print(n)
        yield n

```

This works as expected

for x in cyt.pipe(r1, r2):

3

5
6
7
8
9
source: After all is done

This does not

```
r1 = source()
r2 = foo

for x in cyt.pipe(r1, r2, cyt.take(5)):
    pass

--->

0
1
2
3
4
```
This is not expected behavior. I would expect the post StopIteration statements to work here too. One can argue that `source` has been abruptly terminated, so it's post iteration won't work, but `foo` knows nothing about `source`: it should definitely behave like the iterator has run out.

Thanks!
-Kaushik

A composite layer that rescales the Hamiltonian would add a lot of convenience.  It would rescale the input Hamiltonian to fit within specified ranges, then reverse the rescaling so the energies match the original Hamiltonian.

I currently use the following function to rescale Hamiltonians, with an additional feature that allows clipping large values so that the Hamiltonian doesn't have to be scaled down as much (the tradeoff being that the Hamiltonian is not a faithful rescaling if clipping < 1).

```
def rescale(hh, jj, h_range, j_range, clipping=1.):
    """Rescale the Hamiltonian.

    Clipping truncates values at a certain fraction of the provided range to lessen the
    required compression.
    """

    # Unpack the bounds.
    min_h, max_h = map(float, h_range)
    min_j, max_j = map(float, j_range)

    # Find the natural rescaling factor
    scalars = [
        1.,
        max([h / min_h for h in hh]),
        max([h / max_h for h in hh]),
        max([j / min_j for j in jj.values()]),
        max([j / max_j for j in jj.values()]),
        ]

    scalar = max(scalars)
    # Adjust for clipping
    scalar *= clipping

    hh_scaled = [min_h if h/scalar <= min_h else max_h if h/scalar >= max_h else h/scalar for h in hh]
    jj_scaled = {k: min_j if v/scalar <= min_j else max_j if v/scalar >= max_j else v/scalar for k, v in jj.iteritems()}

    return hh_scaled, jj_scaled, scalar

```

issue_title: We do not need to generate Get  / Set VI Session
issue_body: We currently generate Get  / Set VI Session, we should remove it as it is not used by our drivers.
AutobahnPython itself is a pure Python project, source compatible with Py2/3

First off: I don't know whether the behaviour I'm going to propose is already implemented. If so, it would be nice, if that would be documented.

In a complex application there might be many components depending on each other. In order to fulfill the request of an user, a component might need to access data provided by other components.

For small applications (in regards to traffic/workload), it might be enough to have a single server providing multiple components. If however workload increases, it might be neccessary to split components across multiple server.

In case two or more components are residing on the same server, a regular python method call can be used to invoke the endpoint provided by another component saving a whole roundtrip. If one now needs to scale though, he has to change all method calls into wamp calls again. It would be nice to have one code which can handle both scenarios.

I propose that wamp calls to endpoints registered by the same session, don't result in an actual INVOCATION message but are simply translated to regular python calls. Because this implicitely means that we circumvent the router, which is also responsible for authorization, this should not be the default behaviour. By actively setting a new register option `allow_local_calls`, one can tell Autobahn "that I know what I'm doing and I'm aware of the consequences". CallDetails should be identical to those supplied if a session would have called it self (wth the regular roundtrip to the router).
I realized the field Of View is fixed and the interface does not support changing it. Is there any specific reason for this limitation or this is just not exposed?

issue_title: FYI: PANDAS and 'autoload', and identified problem with numexpr 2.4.4 on Windows
issue_body:
I had a recent issue that took some time to track down. In case this group should experience it. The issue is KNOWN to be numexpr 2.4.4 and on Windows only when using many cores, >16, the problem is that math errors occur due to a race condition. Initially I thought it was PANDAS 0.17 since I was NOT loading numexpr for use. It actually took a while and many error sessions to isolate because the race condition was only occurring about 1 to 1.4% of the time using the exact same input data. The failure always occurred in the first two pandas dataframe rows, values are set to x.x-e315. I learned that PANDAS autoloads any modules they support and autoenables capabilities like numexpr on ordinary expression like df[t]+=1.0 .... So even if you are not crafting numexpr vector operation strings, it will try and succeed for your code.

I could not produce the error with <16 cores. Which made the investigation less than fun.

2.4.6 is out and 'should' correct the issue. I will test soon, in the meantime I dropped back to 2.4.3
FYI
https://github.com/pydata/numexpr/issues/188

issue_title: Ability to set arbitrary conditions on Any()
issue_body:
Hello!  I am a Python user who also noticed quite a bit missing from `unittest.mock`.  I think `mockextras` is 100% moving in the right direction.

I would like to use `mockextras` in all my testing, but first I would like to add a couple of features I see as very generic and useful to everyone, if you agree (I'd love to include these changes in the PYPI package eventually).  The first on my wishlist is the ability to specify arbitrary predicates to an `Any` matcher.

You can see my first pass at a solution here: https://github.com/manahl/mockextras/compare/master...Jdsleppy:jdsleppy/such-that  It enables matchers like:

```python
loud_short_string = (
    Any(str)
    .such_that(lambda s: len(s) < 6)
    .such_that(lambda s: s.upper() == s)
)
assert loud_short_string == "HEY"

positive_int = Any(int).such_that(lambda x: x > 0)
assert positive_int != -2
```

My solution is tested and I think it fits in with the rest of the fluent interface.  I'm happy to battle-harden it in the real world for a time, too, since I _just_ finished the feature.

What do you think?
